prompt: Which of the following typically becomes a LESS effective diversifier when inflation regime shifts from disinflationary to inflationary?
explanation: Long Treasuries hedge equities WHEN stock-bond correlation is negative — which holds in disinflation. In inflation regimes the correlation flips positive: both fall together. 60/40 portfolios calibrated to 1982–2020 underperformed badly in 2022 for this reason.

prompt: Why are P/E multiples typically LOWER in inflationary regimes?
choices: Discount rates rise mechanically with nominal rates AND real earnings are more uncertain, both of which compress the present value of future cash flows.
explanation: Both a rate-mechanics effect and an uncertainty-premium effect. Multiples ARE rates in disguise — that's why every multi-decade equity multiple chart correlates with the inflation/rate regime.

prompt: Pull up Lyn Alden's newsletter archive and find a recent letter discussing inflation regime.
title: Lyn Alden — Newsletter archive
reflectionPrompt: One sentence: what's Alden's current read on whether we're in a new structural inflation regime?
